notes: Editable expense categories + combined one-time expense row. WHAT WAS THERE: the 10 expense categories were a hardcoded EXPENSE_CATEGORIES array whose keys doubled as state field names, iterated directly in ~20 places; Step-3 expense inputs were hardcoded HTML with fixed ids; and each one-off expense rendered as its own row in the projection sheet (Budget page month-by-month table). WHAT CHANGED: (1) Schema v15 adds state.categoryLabels (built-in rename overrides), state.customCategories ([{key,label,color}]), and state.deletedCategories ([builtInKey]); freshState + migrateState seed them. (2) New single accessor getExpenseCats() merges built-ins (minus deleted, with label overrides) plus custom categories; every consumer (inputs read/write, engine per-category series, expense-split donut, budget tubes/actuals, projection rows, retirement need, transaction category tagging, review card) now reads through it instead of the raw array, so a rename/add/delete propagates everywhere from one place. Two previously-unguarded category reduces were hardened with Number()||0 so a freshly-added category with no amount can't produce NaN. (3) Step-3 expenses grid is now rendered dynamically (renderExpenseCatInputs) with an inline pencil (rename via prompt), an x (delete via confirm), and a '+ Add category' button (addExpenseCategory generates a stable exCustom<base36> key and assigns a color from a fixed pool). Built-ins and customs are both deletable; deleting is display/forward-only (zeroes the amount and hides the category; past logged transactions keep their data but lose the label; tx-derived expenses routed to a deleted Misc are safely dropped via an existing guard). (4) One-time expenses now render as a SINGLE 'One-time expenses' row in the projection sheet. Each month cell shows that month's total; a month with 2+ separate one-offs is expandable (tap the total to reveal per-item note+amount, tap again to collapse) via window._toggleOneTimeCell; months with 0 or 1 item render as a normal cell. Scope is display + input-model only: the projection engine's aggregated oneOffExpSeries and all net-worth/savings-chain/retirement math are untouched — the combined row is a presentation regrouping of the same per-month values. KNOWN ITEM (still deferred): current-month savings forecast uses the engine's PLANNED surplus, so a large current-month expense does not yet re-forecast future savings; touches projectSavingsChain and needs double-count verification. NOTE carried from prior build: the Dashboard Liquidity-health card computes its liquid figure from p.cashSeries while the hero Liquidity value uses chain-adjusted cash; unify later if they diverge. Reusable SELF_TEST.js and PROPAGATION_TEST_{1,2,3}.js remain valid; re-run to confirm invariants after adding a custom category.
